title: indigofera kelleri
intro: Indigofera kelleri, also known as Indian indigo, is a flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it beneficial in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Indigofera kelleri include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and act as an antiseptic, among others. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
